summary: Add a nullable column to the users table and backfill it from the existing immutable signup_date. State complexity is high, but the change is reversible (drop the column) and the backfill is idempotent.

reversibility.why: Nullable-column drop is cheap; the backfill is idempotent since signup_date is immutable, but in-flight rollback between phases needs a plan.

blast_radius.why: Users table is central; the change is additive but the backfill touches every row.

compliance_scope.why: No new regulated data surface; adding a derived column does not introduce PII semantics.

user_facing_impact.why: Internal schema change; no UI, no API contract change user-side.

novelty.why: Nullable-column + backfill is a standard playbook; priors apply.

scope_effort.why: One migration, one backfill job, minimal code.

state_complexity.why: Schema change + full-table backfill = persistent state mutation at scale.

operational_risk.why: Backfill can pressure the DB; needs batching and a window; not inherently unsafe but must be planned.

coordination_cost.why: Backend + migration + test authorship within one team.

specialists.name: solution-architect
specialists.why: Decide column type, nullability semantics, and indexing strategy.

specialists.name: migration-engineer
specialists.why: Own the migration + backfill plan, batching strategy, and rollback.

specialists.name: backend-engineer
specialists.why: Implement the migration file and the backfill code.

specialists.name: test-designer
specialists.why: Design unit + integration + migration coverage including idempotency.

phases.name: clarify
phases.why: Confirm intended semantics of the new column and backfill window.

phases.name: design
phases.why: Settle on type/index choice and backfill batching approach.

phases.name: test-strategy
phases.why: Plan migration + integration coverage including rollback and idempotency.

phases.name: test
phases.why: Run migration test against a snapshot and integration coverage against the new column.

phases.name: review
phases.why: Confirm rollback plan and DB impact before prod apply.

rigor_why: Schema change demands a rollback plan and migration test, but no compliance or cross-service amplifiers fire — enforced gates fit, full-rigor ceremony doesn't.
complexity_why: Small code surface but high state-complexity and a full-table backfill place it firmly in mid-range.
open_questions: Table size estimate and acceptable backfill window?

tasks.title: Clarify column semantics and backfill window
Design column type, nullability, and index strategy
Write migration + batched backfill with rollback plan
Implement code paths that use the new column
Run integration + migration test suites against snapshot
Review rollback plan + DB pressure estimate
